BLDC Motor Control
  • Core Module
  • src.core.load_model
  • View page source

src.core.load_model

Load Model Module

This module provides various load models for the motor simulation. The load models generate torque profiles that can be constant, ramping, or variable over time.

Supported load types: - Constant load (fixed torque) - Ramp load (linear change in torque over time) - Variable load (custom time-dependent profile)

author:

Amine Khettat

version:

1.0.0

Added in version 1.0.0: Initial implementation of load models

Classes

ConstantLoad([torque])

Constant load model.

CyclicLoad([offset, amplitude, frequency, phase])

Cyclic load model with sinusoidal profile.

LoadProfile()

Abstract base class for load profiles.

RampLoad([initial, final, duration])

Ramping load model.

VariableLoad([torque_func, time_points, ...])

Variable load model with custom time-dependent profile.
class src.core.load_model.LoadProfile[source]

Bases: ABC

Abstract base class for load profiles.

All load models inherit from this class and implement the get_torque() method to provide time-dependent load torque.

abstractmethod get_torque(time)[source]

Get load torque at specified time.

Parameters:

time (float) – Simulation time [seconds]

Returns:

Load torque [N*m]

Return type:

float

reset()[source]

Reset load profile to initial state.

Not all profiles need resetting, but this method provides a consistent interface.

Return type:

None

class src.core.load_model.ConstantLoad(torque=0.0)[source]

Bases: LoadProfile

Constant load model.

Applies a fixed torque throughout simulation.

Example:
>>> load = ConstantLoad(torque=2.5)
>>> load.get_torque(0.0)
2.5
>>> load.get_torque(10.0)
2.5
__init__(torque=0.0)[source]

Initialize constant load.

Parameters:

torque (float) – Constant load torque [N*m], defaults to 0.0

get_torque(time)[source]

Get constant load torque.

Parameters:

time (float) – Simulation time [seconds] (not used)

Returns:

Load torque [N*m]

Return type:

float

class src.core.load_model.RampLoad(initial=0.0, final=1.0, duration=1.0)[source]

Bases: LoadProfile

Ramping load model.

The load torque ramps linearly from initial value to final value over a specified duration.

Load Profile:

\[\tau(t) = \tau_0 + \frac{(\tau_f - \tau_0)}{t_ramp} * t \quad (0 \leq t \leq t_{ramp})\]
\[\tau(t) = \tau_f \quad (t > t_{ramp})\]
Example:
>>> load = RampLoad(initial=0.0, final=5.0, duration=2.0)
>>> load.get_torque(1.0)  # At t=1s
2.5
>>> load.get_torque(3.0)  # After ramp
5.0
__init__(initial=0.0, final=1.0, duration=1.0)[source]

Initialize ramp load.

Parameters:

  • initial (float) – Initial load torque [N*m], defaults to 0.0

  • final (float) – Final load torque [N*m], defaults to 1.0

  • duration (float) – Ramp duration [seconds], defaults to 1.0

Raises:

ValueError – If duration <= 0

get_torque(time)[source]

Get ramping load torque.

Parameters:

time (float) – Simulation time [seconds]

Returns:

Load torque [N*m]

Return type:

float

class src.core.load_model.VariableLoad(torque_func=None, time_points=None, torque_points=None)[source]

Bases: LoadProfile

Variable load model with custom time-dependent profile.

Allows definition of arbitrary load torque profiles using: - Time-torque paired data points (interpolated) - Custom callback function

Example (with function):
>>> def load_func(t):
...     return 2.0 * np.sin(2*np.pi*t)
>>> load = VariableLoad(torque_func=load_func)
>>> load.get_torque(0.25)
2.0
Example (with time points):
>>> times = [0.0, 1.0, 2.0]
>>> torques = [0.0, 5.0, 2.0]
>>> load = VariableLoad(time_points=times, torque_points=torques)
__init__(torque_func=None, time_points=None, torque_points=None)[source]

Initialize variable load with either function or data points.

Parameters:

  • torque_func (Optional[Callable[[float], float]]) – Custom torque function: f(time) -> torque, optional

  • time_points (Optional[List[float]]) – List of time points [seconds], optional

  • torque_points (Optional[List[float]]) – List of torque values [N*m], optional

Raises:

  • ValueError – If both torque_func and time_points are None

  • ValueError – If time_points and torque_points lengths don’t match

  • ValueError – If time_points not sorted in ascending order

get_torque(time)[source]

Get variable load torque.

Uses linear interpolation if data points are defined, otherwise calls the torque function.

Parameters:

time (float) – Simulation time [seconds]

Returns:

Load torque [N*m]

Return type:

float

Note

For time values beyond the defined range with data points, the value is clamped to the last defined point.

class src.core.load_model.CyclicLoad(offset=0.0, amplitude=1.0, frequency=1.0, phase=0.0)[source]

Bases: LoadProfile

Cyclic load model with sinusoidal profile.

Generates periodic load torque: τ(t) = τ_offset + τ_amplitude * sin(2π*f*t + φ)

Load Profile:

\[\tau(t) = \tau_{offset} + \tau_{amplitude} \cdot \sin(2\pi f t + \varphi)\]
Example:
>>> load = CyclicLoad(offset=1.0, amplitude=2.0, frequency=1.0)
>>> load.get_torque(0.0)
1.0
>>> load.get_torque(0.25)  # At peak
3.0
__init__(offset=0.0, amplitude=1.0, frequency=1.0, phase=0.0)[source]

Initialize cyclic load.

Parameters:

  • offset (float) – Offset torque (mean value) [N*m], defaults to 0.0

  • amplitude (float) – Amplitude of oscillation [N*m], defaults to 1.0

  • frequency (float) – Oscillation frequency [Hz], defaults to 1.0

  • phase (float) – Initial phase [radians], defaults to 0.0

Raises:

ValueError – If frequency < 0

get_torque(time)[source]

Get cyclic load torque.

Parameters:

time (float) – Simulation time [seconds]

Returns:

Load torque [N*m]

Return type:

float
